dsaGuidePhase3Body: Shortlisted students are invited to school-run selection activities — for example interviews, auditions, or trials — depending on the talent area. Each school runs its own schedule; check email and the portal regularly so you do not miss instructions or time slots.

dsaGuidePhase4AfterDeadline: . The order you submit determines how offers are considered in the allocation exercise.

dsaGuidePhase5AfterPsle: , so families see posting and DSA outcomes in the same window. Follow MOE’s published instructions for next steps and any appeals process.
dsaGuideFactsLead: Two policy points parents often overlook when weighing DSA against the mainstream posting exercise.
dsaGuideFactCommitmentBefore1: Once you accept a DSA offer and complete the preference submission as required, your child is committed to that pathway. They will
dsaGuideFactCommitmentStrong1: not participate in the regular Secondary 1 (S1) posting exercise
dsaGuideFactCommitmentAfter: except in exceptional circumstances defined by MOE.
dsaGuideFactPsleBefore: DSA is an early allocation route, but it is
dsaGuideFactPsleMid: of PSLE standards. Your child’s PSLE score must still meet the minimum requirement for the school’s
dsaGuideFactPsleAfter: (and any course eligibility rules) set by MOE for the offer to stand.
dsa_what_is_title: What is the Singapore DSA-Sec Scheme?
dsa_what_is_body: The Direct School Admission (DSA-Sec) scheme allows Primary 6 students to secure a place in their preferred secondary schools based on talents and achievements beyond the PSLE results. This pathway recognizes excellence in diverse areas such as Sports, Performing Arts, STEM, Leadership, and Languages. The 2026 DSA application window typically opens in May. Securing a DSA offer provides students with peace of mind and a guaranteed spot before the PSLE national examinations in November.
dsa_rules_intro: Before proceeding with your application, please be aware of these binding MOE regulations:
dsa_commitment_body: Once a student accepts a DSA offer and the offer is confirmed, they are legally committed to that school. They will not be allowed to participate in the S1 Posting Exercise to choose other schools.
dsa_limits_body: Students may apply to multiple schools. However, once an offer is accepted during the official Acceptance Stage, all other pending applications will be automatically voided.
dsa_assessment_body: Each school maintains unique selection criteria. This often involves trials, auditions, portfolios, or interviews. We recommend checking individual school pages on DSALink for specific 2026 requirements.
dsa_advantage_lead: DSALink is the most comprehensive data aggregator for the Singapore secondary school landscape, designed to help families navigate the complex admission process with precision.
dsa_about_body: DSALink is an independent data platform developed and maintained by Terraflux Studio. All data is aggregated from the Ministry of Education (MOE) SchoolFinder and individual official school portals. This platform is not affiliated with the Ministry of Education, Singapore.
fieldGuide_subtitle: Three focused checklists to get signal from every school visit — culture, programmes, and your DSA conversation.
fieldGuide_card1_body: Look beyond buildings: notice student morale and everyday interactions. Listen for how guides describe peers and teachers when questions go off-script — that reveals the real school vibe.
fieldGuide_card1_tip: Spend a few minutes near arrival areas or the canteen; unchoreographed moments tell you more than a polished deck.
fieldGuide_card2_body: Ask how Applied Learning (ALP) and Lifelong Learning (LLP) programmes work in practice — who can join, how cohorts are formed, and how support extends to different learner profiles.
fieldGuide_card2_tip: Ask whether pathways are equally accessible to new students, and how the school measures outcomes beyond competitions — inclusivity shows up in specifics.
fieldGuide_card3_body: Treat the booth as a short portfolio pitch: summarize evidence, connect achievements to the school’s programme, and end with one precise follow-up that fits your child.
fieldGuide_card3_tip: Bring a one-page summary and your two strongest artifacts; ask what evidence selectors actually weight this cycle.
ohGuide_heroTitle: 2026 Singapore Secondary School Open House: The Expert Field Guide
ohGuide_heroSubtitle: Forum-tested tactics, school-type breakdowns, and 10 questions no brochure will answer — everything you need to get real signal from every campus visit.
ohGuide_prep_heading: Before You Set Foot on Campus
ohGuide_prep_lead: Most families arrive unprepared and leave with glossy brochures and no real information. These four steps flip that.
ohGuide_prep1_body: You cannot visit 147 schools. Map your child's top 3 DSA talent areas against the school database, then filter by PSLE COP band and travel time. Aim for 5–8 schools across two weekends.
ohGuide_prep2_title: Pre-read the school website the night before
ohGuide_prep2_body: Screenshot the DSA talent area page and the ALP/LLP summary. Go in knowing the official line so you can probe the gap between marketing and reality.
ohGuide_prep3_body: Most 2026 open houses require advance registration on the school website. Slots — especially DSA booth sessions — fill up fast. Register at least one week out.
ohGuide_prep4_title: Prepare one sharp question per child's situation
ohGuide_prep4_body: Generic questions get generic answers. Frame yours around your child's actual profile: 'My daughter trains 4× a week in fencing — how does your training schedule fit around Year 1 subjects?' That unlocks real conversation.
ohGuide_types_heading: What Each School Type Actually Shows You
ohGuide_types_lead: IP, SAP, and mainstream O-Level schools run very different open houses. Know what to look for before you walk in.
ohGuide_typeIP_body: Showcase: 6-year pathway, subject combinations at Year 5, overseas programmes, research electives, and university pipeline results. What they often omit: what happens if your child's AL score is borderline for the IP track. Ask directly about minimum PSLE requirements and whether the IP track is guaranteed once DSA is accepted. COP for IP track is generally AL 4–8; confirm the school's specific posting group.
ohGuide_typeSAP_body: Showcase: bilingual excellence, Chinese cultural arts CCAs, Higher Chinese or Chinese Literature as a subject option, and strong alumni networks with links to China scholarships. What to probe: whether the intensive bilingual environment suits your child's mother tongue level, and whether DSA via Chinese cultural arts requires a specific MTL grade for the offer to hold.
ohGuide_typeOLevel_body: Showcase: ALP and LLP programmes (often STEM, digital media, sports excellence), O-Level subject combinations, and CCA depth. These schools often have the widest DSA talent variety — look for schools where the ALP or LLP directly maps to your child's DSA talent area, since that alignment means the programme exists beyond Year 1 onboarding.
ohGuide_checklists_lead: Use all three on every visit. Culture, programme depth, and DSA booth each require a different lens.
ohGuide_questions_lead: These are the questions that separate informed families from the crowd. DSA booth staff and subject teachers — not student guides — are the right people to ask.
ohGuide_q1: What percentage of DSA students are still active in the CCA programme by Year 3?
ohGuide_q2: Has the DSA selection criteria changed for 2026 compared to 2025? What carried the most weight?
ohGuide_q3: If my child is selected via DSA but then finds the CCA schedule unsustainable — what is the process?
ohGuide_q4: Does your school hold information sessions for shortlisted DSA candidates before the official selection date?
ohGuide_q5: What does a 'strong' portfolio submission look like for this specific talent area?
ohGuide_q6: How does the ALP integrate with the normal timetable — is it additional load or does it replace something?
ohGuide_q7: For students who come in via DSA with a non-academic talent, what academic support exists in Year 1?
ohGuide_q8: What is the typical subject combination profile for students in this CCA in Year 4/5?
ohGuide_q9: Are there past DSA students I could contact, or a student panel I could attend?
ohGuide_q10: How does the school handle it if a DSA student's PSLE score meets the minimum posting group but is towards the lower end?
ohGuide_flags_lead: Based on parent forums (KiasuParents, Reddit r/singapore, school parent groups) — patterns that consistently signal whether a school's open house is worth trusting.
ohGuide_green1: Current students break from the script — they volunteer a specific problem the school solved for them.
ohGuide_green2: DSA booth staff quote actual selection criteria with numbers, not 'holistic assessment' vagueness.
ohGuide_green3: The school openly states what happens if a DSA student's academic performance falls — no deflection.
ohGuide_green4: Teachers demonstrate knowing individual students by name, suggesting small CCA cohort sizes.
ohGuide_red1: Every question about workload or CCA schedule is redirected to 'our students are very well-rounded' without specifics.
ohGuide_red2: The DSA booth cannot tell you how many students were selected via that talent area in 2025.
ohGuide_red3: Student ambassadors can only describe the programme they're in — no one from another CCA knows anything about other DSA areas.
ohGuide_red4: The open house tour avoids the actual CCA facilities — you see classrooms and canteen but not the studio, court, or lab.
ohGuide_after_heading: After the Visit: Converting Impressions into Action
ohGuide_after_lead: The hour after you leave campus is your most valuable window. Don't waste it.
ohGuide_after1_body: Rate culture, DSA programme depth, and your gut feeling on a 1–5 scale while you're still on the way home. Ask your child independently: 'Did you feel like you belonged there?' Their answer matters more than the ranking.
ohGuide_after2_body: Once you're home, pull up the PSLE COP dashboard. Check whether your child's projected AL score gives a safe margin, a borderline entry, or is out of range. A school you loved at the open house but can't realistically post to is an emotional risk, not a strategy.
ohGuide_after3_title: Update your shortlist, not your portfolio
ohGuide_after3_body: Use your notes to re-rank your 5–8 schools before the May application window opens. Don't start rewriting your child's portfolio based on one open house conversation — the portfolio should reflect real, sustained achievement, not a rushed repositioning.
ohGuide_footerNote: Guidance compiled from MOE official sources, KiasuParents community threads, and parent experience reports. Always verify current requirements on each school's official website and the MOE DSA-Sec Portal.
ohGuide_featured_lead: Verified open house dates sourced from school websites and the Singapore school calendar. Confirmed schools link directly to their 2026 open house page; unconfirmed schools link to the admissions page.
